item.title: 菲律宾发生里氏7.8级地震，造成至少32人死亡、建筑物倒塌并引发海啸
item.description:
DAVAO, Philippines (AP) — An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake hit the southern Philippines on Monday, killing at least 32 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in ruined buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.
Several mostly low-rise buildings collapsed or sustained heavy damages in the hard-hit city of General Santos. Tsunami damage was reported in at least one southern coastal village. Smaller waves were measured in Indonesia and Palau and as far away as southern Japan.
In a video shared by Mahayahay Elementary School on social media, school children were seen screaming and crying when an offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines early Monday.
The quake also triggered a landslide in Glan, a municipality in the province of Sarangani, that killed 13 villagers, Rene Punzalan, a provincial disaster-mitigation official, told the DZBB radio network. Four other villagers died in Sarangani, he said.
The major earthquake was the strongest to hit the Philippines this year, Teresito Bacolcol, the director of the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology, said. He warned people to seek advice before returning to damaged buildings and houses, which could collapse due to aftershocks.
The United States, a treaty ally of the Philippines, said it was coordinating with Manila and was ready to support Philippine response efforts. France and New Zealand also expressed support.
“Our pickup truck suddenly jerked and I thought we had a flat tire,” Rod Sosmeña, a regional director of the Office of Civil Defense, told The Associated Press from General Santos, where he was traveling when the quake struck at 7:37 a.m.
“The shaking was very strong and people dashed out of houses into the streets,” Sosmeña said.
More than 100 students in uniforms and a dozen teachers had gathered for a flag-raising ceremony in a coconut tree-ringed grade school compound in the rural town of Malita in Davao Occidental province when the ground shook, turning the first day of school after a two-month summer break into chaos.
“Their excitement on the first day of school turned to trauma,” school principal Rosavel Cachuela told the AP.
Some of the young students screamed in panic and wept but mostly remained seated and still, preventing any injuries, Cachuela said, adding that a motorcycle was damaged when a shed crumbled to the ground.
At least 12 people remained missing in General Santos, a port city of more than 700,000 people and a regional hub for the tuna export industry. Search and rescue teams scrambled to find people who may have been trapped in a supermarket, a warehouse, a grade school, and other small buildings that either collapsed or were severely damaged, officials said.
The international airport in General Santos was temporarily shut due to the earthquake and 17 domestic flights were canceled, the Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines said.
The quake was centered at sea off Mindanao, the second most populous island in the Philippine archipelago. According to Bacolcol, the quake occurred at a depth of 33 kilometers (20 miles), about 32 kilometers (20 miles) southwest of Maasim town in Sarangani province.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. ordered the cancellation of classes and directed disaster-response agencies to immediately get to work in quake-hit provinces, saying “the national government is moving and we will not leave Mindanao behind.”
A magnitude 7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and sparking a tsunami warning on some regional coasts.
The Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said the threat of a tsunami largely passed about five hours after the quake. Philippine officials also lifted a tsunami warning by mid-afternoon. Six shanties on stilts were damaged in a coastal village in Zamboanga del Sur province due to the quake and taller waves, officials said.
Aside from the landslide in Sarangani, most of the other deaths were caused by collapsing buildings and falling debris, including in a damaged mosque, in the southern provinces of South Cotabato and Davao Occidental, and on Balut Island, according to Sosmeña and another disaster-mitigation official, Ednar Dayanghirang.
The DZRH radio network in Manila reported that a four-story commercial building where its provincial station was located partly collapsed and staffers dashed to the ground floor without injuries.
Waves of 1 meter (3 feet) were monitored in the provinces of Sultan Kudarat and Sarangani. A 1.4-meter (4.6-foot) wave hit at one time in Kiamba town, Bacolcol said.
The quake was also felt in Malaysia’s Sabah state on Borneo island. Sabah is just a boat ride away from southern Philippines. An 83-centimeter (2.7-feet) tsunami was measured by a gauge off Indonesia’s Sulawesi island, and the PTWC said 30-centimeter (1-foot) waves were measured in Palau.
Waves up to 20 centimeters (7.8 inches) were detected on the remote Japanese island of Chichijima and the central Japanese town of Kushimoto, the Japan Meteorological Agency said.
The Philippines, one of the world’s most disaster-prone countries, is often hit by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions due to its location on the Pacific “Ring of Fire,” an arc of seismic faults around the ocean.
The archipelago is also lashed by about 20 typhoons and tropical storms each year.
Gomez reported from Manila, Philippines. Associated Press writers Edna Tarigan in Jakarta, Indonesia, Eileen Ng in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, Mari Yamaguchi in Tokyo and global executive producer Kiko Rosario in Bangkok contributed to this report.

item.title: 以色列和伊朗的贸易打击可能使该地区重新陷入全面战争
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Israel and Iran traded fire Monday in their first attacks since the U.S. struck a ceasefire with Tehran two months ago. The Iranian military said hours later that it was halting offensive operations as the renewed hostilities threatened to drag the Middle East back intoa full-scale war.
The U.S. told Iran there would be no more attacks by Israel if Iran halted its missile strikes, and that Israel has agreed to halt attacks for now, according to a regional official who spoke on condition of anonymity because they were not authorized to speak to the media.
The White House and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Since the U.S. and Israel began striking Iran on Feb. 28, the war hasshaken the global economy, driven energy prices up around the world and made many basics,including food, more expensive. Officials have been unable to turnthe April ceasefireinto a deal to permanently end the conflict.
During the truce, Iran has maintained its stranglehold on theStrait of Hormuz— a crucial passage for the world’s oil and natural gas whose closure was the primary reason global fuel prices skyrocketed. Israel has continued to strike Hezbollah, Iran’s ally in Lebanon, andpushed deeper into that country. And on Monday, Yemen’s Houthi rebels, another Iranian ally, fired at Israel and warned they would target Israel-affiliated ships in the Red Sea.
With little apparent progress in peace talks, Israel and Iran exchanging fire, and theHouthis joining the fight, the risk of the war fully erupting again appeared higher than at any point since the ceasefire.
In the wake of the new attacks, U.S. President Donald Trump wrote online: “Israel and Iran must immediately stop ‘shooting.’”
Shortly after, the Iranian military’s joint command issued its statement. It said further “aggression and hostile acts” by Israel and its supporters, including in southern Lebanon, would be met with “much more severe and crushing measures than before.”
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said Monday night that Israel’s military will continue to operate against Hezbollah in Lebanon. He warned that any Iranian strikes in retaliation for Israeli attacks on Hezbollah would be “met with great force.”
Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed concern Monday over the surge in violence. In a post on X, Sharif urged all parties to “exercise restraint and give peace a little more chance.”
Two regional officials said diplomatic efforts were underway to salvage the ceasefire.
Officials from Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Pakistan and Qatar urged the Trump administration to pressure Israel to halt strikes onIran and Beirut. They also urged Iranian officials to stop attacks on Israel, the officials said. Both spoke on condition of anonymity because they were not authorized to speak to reporters.
Trump said talks were ongoing for a ceasefire between Israel and Iran, though he gave no details.
Iran launched waves of attacks on Israel on Monday, and Israel launched strikes on central and western Iran. It was their first exchange of fire since the ceasefire.
Iranian state media reported at least 15 people were wounded after explosions sounded in Tehran and other cities. There were no immediate reports of fatalities.
The semiofficial Fars and Mehr news agencies said Israeli strikes hit a petrochemical factory in the city of Mahshahr. They did not elaborate on any damage. The Israeli military confirmed the strike on the plant, saying it targeted sites that produce materials for ballistic missiles. Israel said it also targeted truck-based missile launchers.
Israel said its strikes were in response to an Iranian missile attack. Tehran warned Sunday that it would retaliate after Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs without warning. When Israel struck back, Iran fired again.
Explosions could be heard in central Israel as air defenses sought to intercept incoming Iranian fire. Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard said it had targeted two military bases in Israel.
Iran blamed the United States for the escalation.
“No one believes that the Israeli regime would take any action without coordination with the United States,” Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei told journalists in Tehran.
The White House did not immediately respond to messages about Israel’s strikes.
Trump and Netanyahu launched the war in aclosely coordinated attack, with Israeli officials proudly boasting of unprecedented “shoulder to shoulder” cooperation.
The conflict reached 100 days on Monday, and the two leaders have moved in opposite directions, withtensions sometimes spilling out into the open.
Netanyahu appears to have openly defied Trump with the strike Sunday in Beirut and subsequent attacks in Iran. Trump has voiced his displeasure with Israel, including belittling Netanyahu by declaring to the Financial Times that “I call all the shots.”
Their differences appear to be rooted in each leader’s domestic considerations. Netanyahu faces elections this fall and is under public pressure to strike back against ongoing Hezbollah attacks on northern Israel. He also is wary of appearing too subservient to Trump.
The U.S. president, meanwhile, also faces elections — for Congress in November — and is eager to end a war that has jolted the global economy and raised prices for consumers.
Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels claimed an attack on Israel on Monday and said Israel-affiliated vessels would again be a target in the Red Sea, putting the waterway in danger along with the Gulf of Aden and the narrow Bab el-Mandeb Strait connecting them. The statement from Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree was broadcast on the Houthis’ al-Masirah satellite news channel.
The Houthis made a similar threat during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip and launched attacks that killed at least nine mariners and sank four ships. They often targeted vessels with tangential or no ties to Israel.
The assaults upendedshipping in the Red Sea, through which about $1 trillion of goods passed each year before the war.
This story has been updated to correct the day the Iran war started to Feb. 28.
Lidman reported from Tel Aviv, Israel; Associated Press writers Munir Ahmed in Islamabad; Matthew Lee in Washington; Michelle L. Price in Bridgewater, New Jersey; Elena Becatoros in Athens, Greece; Samy Magdy in Cairo; and Josef Federman in Jerusalem contributed to this report.

item.title: 当局称，纽约宾州车站发生刺伤事件，造成 6 人受伤，一名嫌疑人已被拘留
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——当局称，周日晚间，纽约宾州车站内发生一起刺伤事件，造成六人受伤，不到一天前，预计将有数千名球迷前往邻近的麦迪逊广场花园观看 NBA 总决赛第三场比赛。
袭击发生在晚上 7 点左右，一名嫌疑人被拘留。位于全国最繁忙的交通枢纽之一。麦迪逊广场花园下方庞大的铁路综合体为美国铁路公司、长岛铁路、新泽西州交通和纽约市地铁线路提供服务，每天有数十万通勤者和旅客使用。
据该市消防部门称，医护人员发现一名受重伤的受害者、两名中度受伤的受害者和两名轻伤的受害者。所有五人都被送往贝尔维尤医院。另一人的情况尚不清楚，已被送往另一家医院。
当局没有立即公布导致刺伤事件或受害者是否成为袭击目标的详细信息。
到周日晚些时候，眼前的混乱已经被宾夕法尼亚车站熟悉的场景所取代。旅客推着行李经过 5 号和 6 号轨道附近的一个用绳子围起来的区域，那里的地板上仍然残留着医用手套、废弃的绷带和血迹。
当纽约准备数十年来最大的体育赛事之一时，暴力事件爆发。周一晚上纽约尼克斯队和圣安东尼奥马刺队之间的对决将是自 1999 年以来第一场在麦迪逊广场花园举行的 NBA 总决赛比赛，预计将吸引大量观众前往球场。
唐纳德·特朗普总统表示，他计划参加全国电视转播的比赛，这促使特勤局、纽约警察局和麦迪逊广场花园及周边街区周围其他机构进行了广泛的安全保护。当局没有立即表明刺伤事件是否会影响比赛的安全计划。
美铁警方表示，他们正在调查这起刺伤事件，并已拘留一名嫌疑人。当局没有立即公布嫌疑人的身份或提供有关潜在指控的信息。

item.title: 俄亥俄州街头节日附近发生枪击事件，造成 12 人受伤，警方正在搜寻嫌疑人
item.description:
俄亥俄州在一个繁忙的街头节日附近开枪的嫌疑人搜查工作一直持续到周日，周末发生的枪击事件造成 12 人受伤，导致人群在历史悠久的托莱多街区争先恐后寻找掩护。
托莱多警察局周日表示，十几名受害者——从青少年到 60 多岁——均情况稳定。目前尚未逮捕任何人，当局也未发现任何潜在嫌疑人。
周六，枪声在老西区音乐节附近爆发，这是一年一度的夏季聚会，街道上遍布着维多利亚风格的房屋。社交媒体上发布的视频显示，人们在枪声中奔跑，医务人员在摆满活动帐篷和餐车的公园里救治伤员。
托莱多副警察局长乔·赫弗南表示，至少有两人开枪，“可能是在互相开枪”。
随着对枪手的搜寻工作不断进行，卢卡斯县检察官朱莉娅·贝茨发誓“正义将会迅速而有力。”
贝茨在一份声明中表示：“那些因暴力事件而受到惊吓、创伤或伤害的人将继续站在我们努力的最前线。我以前也曾感到愤怒，但这是针对我个人的。”“这是我的家。这些是我的朋友和邻居。这不好。”
托莱多警方周日在社交媒体上发布的简短更新中表示，调查人员正在继续取得进展，并正在追踪多条线索。当局敦促参加节日的人们提供任何可能瞥见嫌疑人的照片或视频。
数百人参加了托莱多历史街区一年一度的为期两天的庆祝活动，活动包括现场音乐、食品摊贩、家庭旅游和购物。托莱多位于俄亥俄州西北部，靠近伊利湖西缘，距底特律西南约 55 英里（90 公里）。
“就暴力而言，这太过分了，对吧？”托莱多警方丹·格肯中尉周六表示。“十二个人被枪杀，这是我去过的最多的一个场景。我去过很多场景，但这太夸张了。”
周日，音乐节的其余活动被取消。组织者表示，继续整个周末“既不富有同情心，也不负责任，也不可能”。
该节日在一份声明中表示：“我们对那些在老西区音乐节上受伤的人感到心碎。”
受害者年龄从14岁到61岁不等，其中大多数20岁出头。消防队长艾利森·阿姆斯特朗表示，由于道路封闭和离开音乐节的交通，紧急救援人员很难将伤者送往医院，但所有伤者都在一个小时内被运送完毕。
当凯文·贝里（Kevin Berry）听到枪声响起时，他正和朋友坐在附近的植物园里听现场音乐。
当贝里回头看时，他看到一把枪被扔在距离他不到 50 英尺（15 米）的地方。已经在现场参加节日的官员立即做出了回应。
贝里接受过医疗培训并在海军服役，他四处寻找可能需要帮助的人，发现至少有五人受枪伤。
该市安全总监乔治·克拉尔 (George Kral) 表示，老西区音乐节是托莱多最具标志性的节日之一。
“遗憾的是，这样的事情不得不毁了它，”克拉尔说。
俄亥俄州枪击事件是周末美国至少发生的两起大规模枪支暴力事件之一。
警方称，密苏里州堪萨斯城周六早些时候发生枪击事件，造成九名成年人受伤。派往市中心以南约 10 英里（16 公里）地区的警官发现一大群人群正在散去。警方表示，没有一名受害者有生命危险，也没有嫌疑人被拘留。

item.title: 诉讼试图阻止 UFC 在白宫南草坪举行的特朗普生日之战
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——一项联邦诉讼试图阻止即将在白宫南草坪举行的 UFC 综合格斗比赛，这场比赛是为庆祝美国总统唐纳德·特朗普 80 岁生日而举行，也是庆祝美国建国 250 周年的一部分。
公共诚信项目代表两名弗吉尼亚州居民于周六提交的文件称，特朗普政府对 6 月 14 日活动的授权是非法的。该诉讼称，这种批准违反了国家公园管理局禁止在联邦公园用地上举办体育赛事的规定，国会不同意这座高耸的拱门俯瞰活动空间，并且在施工前没有进行环境审查。
唐纳德·特朗普总统以其几十年来作为知名房地产开发商的身份，正在监督华盛顿特区周边的美化项目，例如修复城市喷泉和重新粉刷国家广场上的倒影池。（美联社视频：内森·埃尔格伦）
原告律师布伦丹·巴卢（Brendan Ballou）表示：“这从根本上说是一种私人、商业、腐败的行为，利用我们最神圣的国家纪念碑谋取私利。”“这就是引发这起诉讼的原因。”
白宫在一份声明中表示，这一法律挑战是“一种蓄意阻挠、毫无根据且拖延”的行为，旨在阻止特朗普主办这场比赛，该活动“与白宫在南草坪举办的其他各种活动以及全年在椭圆形和国家广场举行的适当许可的活动没有什么不同。”
UFC周日没有立即回应置评请求。
工作人员正在南草坪上搭建一个八角形的笼子。特朗普表示，已完成的 UFC 项目将“在白宫前门外建造一个拥有 5,000 个座位的竞技场”。附近椭圆形公园内还将设置额外的大屏幕来播放比赛，UFC 表示计划发放多达 85,000 张免费门票，以容纳两个地点的观众。
八边形及周边建筑是特朗普引领的白宫建筑热潮中的最新项目。

item.title: 特朗普否认伊朗背叛了他“不再发动新战争”的竞选信息
item.description:
新泽西州布里奇沃特（美联社）——唐纳德·特朗普总统驳斥了这样一种观点，即今年对伊朗发动的战争背叛了他在再次竞选白宫时反复强调的“不再发动新战争”的说法。
特朗普周日在美国全国广播公司（NBC）“会见媒体”节目中接受采访时表示，他“不能保证”如果他重新执政，不会发生战争。
“首先，我没有保证不会发生战争。我为什么要建立世界上最强大的军队？”特朗普说道。
特朗普还为一项现已废弃的 18 亿美元基金的计划进行了辩护，该基金本来可以补偿这位共和党总统的盟友，他还重申了他毫无根据的指控，称加州周二初选的旷日持久的计票工作存在大规模欺诈行为。当他对 NBC 的克里斯汀·韦尔克 (Kristen Welker) 的抵制感到沮丧时，他突然结束了采访。
在2024年的竞选活动中，特朗普多次将他的民主党对手描绘成好战分子，并表示他是一位“不会发动新战争”并将带来一个和平时代的总统。
但特朗普周五在威斯康星州录制的全国广播公司采访中表示，作为候选人，“我没有承诺任何事情。”
“我不喜欢这些无休止的战争。这不是一场无休止的战争。我们已经这样做了三个月，”他在谈到2月28日开始的与伊朗的战争时说道。
特朗普表示，他正在“为世界服务”和“为我们的国家服务”，因为他必须阻止伊朗拥有核武器。但在采访的其他地方，特朗普重复了一个矛盾的信息，他说美国去年的袭击“摧毁了”伊朗核设施。
他还为自己在第一任期内退出民主党总统巴拉克·奥巴马与伊朗核协议的决定进行了辩护，他曾严厉批评该协议，但没有就他承诺达成的“更好协议”进行谈判。
“做这些事情需要数年时间，”特朗普说。
加州众所周知的长期计票工作一直吸引着选举阴谋论，自周二选举以来，特朗普在没有证据的情况下声称民主党正在操纵选举。特朗普任命的洛杉矶最高联邦检察官周五表示，他的办公室已启动“多项选举舞弊调查”。
最新统计的倾向民主党的邮寄选票已经蚕食了特朗普首选的州长和洛杉矶市长候选人的得票总数。尽管特朗普经常表示，在计票较晚时投票总数发生变化是欺诈的迹象，但这只是计票过程缓慢的反映。
特朗普在采访中不断声称这是“作弊”和“选举被操纵”的迹象，当韦尔克敦促他提供证据来支持这一点时，特朗普变得越来越沮丧。
“我所要做的就是看看。我所要做的就是看看，”特朗普说。
“但这不是证据，”韦尔克回答道。
“我会听。我会听人们的意见。让我们看看会发生什么，”特朗普回答道。
特朗普为司法部表示现已放弃设立 17.76 亿美元“反武器化基金”的计划进行辩护，该计划是解决特朗普因纳税申报表泄露而对美国国税局提起的诉讼的和解协议的一部分。
代理司法部长托德·布兰奇周三表示，该部门正在取消该计划。这一消息是在该计划被法官暂停后发布的，民主党人和一些共和党人都表示，他们担心该基金缺乏监督以及一月份向参与者支付款项的可能性。2021 年 12 月 6 日，国会大厦发生骚乱。
特朗普告诉美国全国广播公司（NBC），他认为该基金是“一个好主意”，如果不获得批准，他将“感到失望”。
当被问及他是否认为 1 月 6 日袭击警察的人应该得到赔偿时，特朗普说，“我不想这么说，但我必须看到这一点。”然后，他开始对骚乱和冲击国会大厦的人做出毫无根据的虚假指控。特朗普于 2025 年 1 月上任第一天就对 1 月 6 日期间被起诉的 1,500 多人进行了全面赦免。
NBC 的采访是在威斯康星州奇珀瓦福尔斯进行的，随后特朗普准备在与农民举行的圆桌会议上发表讲话。由于一阵阵大雨落在录音所在谷仓的金属屋顶上，采访多次被打断，有时很难听清楚。
最后，韦尔克就和解基金以及他对加州选举的主张向特朗普施压。特朗普提高了声音，开始称韦尔克和媒体“不诚实”，攻击她的可信度，并抱怨他所谓的“虚假、肮脏的媒体”。
当韦尔克试图转换话题时，特朗普继续说下去，两人之间发生了交叉谈话。特朗普结束采访时说：“我们就此结束吧。”他摘下麦克风，对韦尔克说：“谢谢你，亲爱的。祝你玩得开心。”他说他给了采访足够的时间，然后站起来走开了。
韦尔克在广播中表示，她在周六与特朗普进行了交谈，特朗普同意降雨造成了并发症，并表示他将来会再次接受采访。

item.title: 尼西亚·拉曼 (Nithya Raman) 和斯宾塞·普拉特 (Spencer Pratt) 争夺洛杉矶市长决选席位
item.description:
洛杉矶（美联社）——加州初选几天后，尼西亚·拉曼 (Nithya Raman) 和斯宾塞·普拉特 (Spencer Pratt) 仍在等待，看看谁能在 11 月份的洛杉矶市长决选中与现任凯伦·巴斯 (Karen Bass) 竞争。
周日的选举还为时过早，因为计票显示拉曼自周二投票结束并开始计票以来首次升至第二位，落后于巴斯。这使得进步的市议会成员拉曼领先于《山丘》中的前真人秀名人普拉特。
拉曼一直排名第三，但自周二以来洛杉矶选举官员提供的每一次更新，她都比普拉特获得了更多的选票。
加州的计票过程是出了名的缓慢，因为州法律实际上规定了长时间的计票。选票将邮寄给每位符合资格的选民，如果选票在选举日之前盖上邮戳并在 7 天内到达选举办公室，则选票将被计入在内。
与加利福尼亚州的其他县一样，洛杉矶大致按照收到的顺序处理和统计邮寄选票，因此最后返回的选票就是最后统计的选票。
周二晚上，投票结束后，洛杉矶公布了提前退回并已经处理的邮寄选票以及当天投票的结果。从那时起，该县一直在处理和发布后来到达的邮寄选票的结果。
选举数据显示，大量民主党人保留了邮寄选票，并在竞选的最后几天将选票退回，这有助于解释为什么巴斯和拉曼自初选之日起计票结果一直好于普拉特。
市长竞选是无党派的，因此选票上没有一位候选人的名字旁边有党派标识。拉曼和巴斯都是民主党人，而普拉特是共和党人。
在选举之夜，巴斯领先普拉特 4.4 个百分点，普拉特又领先拉曼 8.1 个百分点。从那时起，巴斯对普拉特的领先优势已扩大到近 8 个百分点，而拉曼现在领先普拉特约 0.4 个百分点，即 3,100 票。美联社估计，还有不到 15 万张选票需要清点。
计票速度缓慢，导致一些共和党人在没有提供证据的情况下声称存在欺诈行为，其中包括总统唐纳德·特朗普，他表示司法部将进行调查。
总统表示，该州的民主党人在某种程度上作弊，以至于他支持的两名候选人——州长竞选中的普拉特和共和党人史蒂夫·希尔顿——将从前两名席位中被剔除，因此没有资格参加 11 月的大选。民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉 (Xavier Becerrah) 已在州长竞选中晋级大选，但美联社尚未公布第二名的位置。希尔顿在以第二名候选人身份晋级大选的竞争中领先民主党人汤姆·斯泰尔比 4.3 个百分点，尽管自选举之夜以来，他的领先优势几乎缩小了一半。
洛杉矶大选很可能是对巴斯领导力的公投，无论她面对的是拉曼还是普拉特。但两人在竞选中的出发点却截然不同。
普拉特是一名保守派，他将对这座由民主党主导的城市的自由主义治理提出更积极的挑战。他将减少无家可归者作为其竞选活动的一个关键部分，并严厉批评巴斯在 2025 年 1 月太平洋帕利塞兹社区发生野火期间的领导，这场野火摧毁了他的房屋和数千人的房屋。由于他的名气，他的候选资格引起了极大的关注，但目前还不清楚这种关注是否会转化为足够的选票来进入决选。
与此同时，拉曼正在从左路挑战巴斯。她承诺加快住房建设，带回娱乐业就业岗位，并改善这座以肮脏街道和弯曲路面而闻名的城市的服务。她在美国民主社会主义者的支持下当选为市议会成员，尽管该组织并未在市长竞选中正式表示支持。在她早些时候支持巴斯连任后，她在最后一刻的候选资格令人惊讶。

item.title: 尼克斯队球迷唐纳德·特朗普返回纽约支持他的球队
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — There was a time whenDonald Trumpwas just another celebrity sitting courtside at New York Knicks games. He was famous, but not yet flanked by Secret Service agents or defined by the politics that have left him deeply unpopular in his hometown.
Now, more than a decade after attending his last Knicks game at Madison Square Garden, Trump ismaking a rare trip back to New York Cityas president to cheer for them in Game 3 of the NBA Finals against the San Antonio Spurs on Monday night. Invited by Knicks owner James Dolan, he will be the first sitting president to attend an NBA Finals game.
The Knicks are seeking their first championship since 1973, when Trump was 26 and a relative newcomer to the family real estate business that vaulted him to wealth and fame. Two years after that triumph, the team’s owners at the time hired him as a consultant as they looked to sell the arena.
Trump has been to more major sporting events than any of his predecessors, including theSuper BowlandDaytona 500, golf’sRyder Cupin the New York City suburbs, where he was cheered, and last year’sU.S. Open men’s tennis championshipin Queens, where he was booed and blamed for long security lines.
On June 14, when he turns 80 while wrestling with myriad crises including thewar with Iran,economic uneaseandcourt rulingsblunting his agenda, he will host a UFC fight on White House grounds. Trump also has expressed interest in attending soccer’s World Cup, which kicks off this week across the United States, Mexico and Canada.
Trump is an avid sports fan, but the affinity he professes for the Knicks is different.
It speaks to the Republican president’s identity as a New Yorker and harkens to a bygone era where a front-row seat at a Knicks game was a chance for him and other boldface names to see and be seen.
In a city whose wealthy gatekeepers largely turned their noses at Trump’s brash personality and playboy image in the 1990s and 2000s, the Garden’s Celebrity Row was one club where he felt at home.
“I’ve been a Knick fan for a long time,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office last week, a day after New York rallied to win Game 1. “I watched that end of the game and they were dominant — really amazing.”
After another win Friday in San Antonio, the Knicks head home with a 2-0 lead in the best-of-seven series. They have won a remarkable 13 straight playoff games and last lost on April 23, uniting the city in a way unseen since the Knicks went to the NBA Finals twice in the 1990s.
Enter Trump. He returns to the Knicks zeitgeist not as the tabloid curiosity who once sat shoulder to shoulder with the late John F. Kennedy Jr. at a game in 1999, but as a president who is disliked by a majority of the city’s Democratic voters.
Trump, who gave up his lifelong New York residency for Florida in 2019, is making his first trip to New York City since he spoke at the United Nations in September. In 2024, he went on trial in the city and wasconvicted of 34 felony countsrelated to hush money paid on his behalf during his 2016 campaign.
Knicks fans, though, do not seem to be concerned so much with his politics, but that his attendance — and the hoopla accompanying it — could mess up the team’s momentum. The Knicks said people going to the gameshould arrive at least two hours before tipofffor airport-style security screening.
“Why does Donald Trump always have to ruin a good thing?” U.S. Rep. Hakeem Jeffries of New York, an avid Knicks fan and the House Democratic leader, told CNN. “Like, literally, the Knicks haven’t been in the NBA finals for 27 years. The city is trying to celebrate this. We’ve embraced this team, and this guy has to inject himself.”
Mayor Zohran Mamdani, a Democrat who struck up a cordial relationship with Trump after the two met in November, was more inviting.
“We’re excited to welcome anyone and everyone who’s rooting for the Knicks in this moment,” said Mamdani, who will also be at the game — albeit, not with Trump.
Last week, as Trump began floating the idea of attending a game, New York magazine published an article, “Is Trump Really a Knicks Fan? An Investigation.” The story, filled with pictures of Trump at Knicks games from 1991 to 2014, described him as a “textbook example of a celebrity bandwagon fan.”
“Before he ever ran for office, he was a big Knicks fan,” Silver told reporters last week. “I’ve been with the league for a long time. I was there at many Knicks games with him in the old days.”
Trump and the Knicks came into existence the same year, 1946.
His affiliation with the team — at least in the public record — dates to 1975 when he acted as a real estate adviser to the then-owners of the Knicks and Madison Square Garden, who were looking to sell the building known in a bit of Trump-style branding as “The World’s Most Famous Arena.”
Trump claimed to reporters at the time that two groups of “Arab oil interests” were interested in paying $50 million to $75 million. But the arena’s leadership passed on the idea, saying it was “not conceivable” to make such a deal during the Middle East oil crisis raging at the time.
Trump was not much of a known entity when the Knicks won their only championships in 1970 and 1973.
By the time they rebounded in the 1990s, Trump was front and center, taking his then-wife Marla Maples to Game 3 of the NBA Finals in 1994 and his current wife, first lady Melania Trump, to Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals in 1999. In between, he added to his Knicks fan bona fides with a cameo in the Knicks-themed Whoopi Goldberg film “Eddie” in 1996.
Back then, Trump was a more of a mythic figure than a consequential one, known as much for the women he dated and married as the buildings he built.
But just as those Knicks came up short in the NBA Finals against Hakeem Olajuwon and the Houston Rockets and David Robinson and the Spurs, Trump was running into problems of his own. His business empire was in disarray after his casinos fell into financial trouble and his airline, Trump Shuttle, went out of business.
Like the Knicks, Trump went into rebuilding mode and charted a new course: reality TV with NBC’s “The Apprentice” and “Celebrity Apprentice,” and then, politics. On a Knicks TV broadcast in 2010, he hinted at a possible presidential run.
That same year, as the Knicks struggled to recapture the magic of the 1990s, Trump recorded a video trying to persuade LeBron James to join the team.
“The real winners of the world want to be here,” Trump told him.

item.title: 国家队表示，克里斯蒂安·埃里克森在丹麦队比赛中再次摔倒后“意识清醒”
item.description:
周日，克里斯蒂安·埃里克森在代表丹麦国家队比赛时再次倒在球场上，目前意识清醒，正在医院接受进一步检查，这一可怕的场景让人想起五年前欧洲锦标赛上他的心脏骤停。
电视画面显示，在丹麦欧登塞自然能源公园举行的丹麦对阵乌克兰的国际友谊赛中，这位34岁的中场球员在第65分钟进行无球跑动时双手捂住胸口。
在下一张电视图像中，埃里克森仰面躺在地上，周围都是一脸忧心忡忡的球员。有人看到乌克兰教练组挥手示意医务人员上场。
几分钟之内，丹麦足协在 X 上发帖称埃里克森“意识清醒，在这种情况下表现良好”。
10分钟后，足协发表了一份更全面的声明，丹麦队医莫滕·博森表示：“克里斯蒂安做得很好，他自己走出了球场。在我看来，起搏器的反应是应有的。”
“他一度失去知觉，但很快就恢复了知觉，我们很快就与他取得了联系。”
博森表示，埃里克森“现在将在医院接受进一步检查，以确定导致事件发生的原因。”
“我们正在与他和医院的医生保持联系，”博森补充道。“但是克里斯蒂安做得很好，他让我向所有球员转达他的问候，并告诉他们他一切都好。”
第 79 分钟，裁判正式宣布放弃比赛——丹麦队 2-1 领先，埃里克森倒地后没有进一步比赛——比赛官员与教练和球员进行了交谈。
当埃里克森接受治疗时，人群中最初一片安静，然后体育场周围响起了“埃里克森，埃里克森”的口号。
比赛取消后，两队球员在半场围成一圈，围绕着两位教练。人们看到教练们正在与球员们交谈。随后，球队在球场上走动，赢得了人群的掌声，但一些球员显然很沮丧。
埃里克森是丹麦有史以来最伟大的球员之一，在 2021 年丹麦在哥本哈根对阵芬兰的欧洲锦标赛小组赛首场比赛中，他面朝前倒地，几个月后，他安装了植入式心律转复除颤器。
在那场震惊足坛的事件中，埃里克森在博森领导的长时间现场医疗治疗中苏醒过来，并表示后来被告知他“离开这个世界五分钟了”。
259天后，埃里克森在英超布伦特福德获得合同后恢复了职业球员生涯。在 2022-25 赛季在曼联效力一段时间后，他加盟德国俱乐部沃尔夫斯堡直至 2026-27 赛季。
沃尔夫斯堡在 X 的帖子中表示，埃里克森已被送往欧登塞大学医院，俱乐部正在关注事态发展。
“祝你一切顺利，早日康复，克里斯蒂安，”沃尔夫斯堡说道。
曼联还发表了一份声明，表示“对丹麦关于克里斯蒂安·埃里克森的最新消息感到鼓舞”。
曼联表示：“在我们等待进一步消息的同时，俱乐部正在向克里斯蒂安和埃里克森家族送去力量和爱。”
丹麦和乌克兰均未获得世界杯参赛资格。

item.title: 曾效力过三支 NBA 冠军球队的公牛队长期解说员史黛西·金 (Stacey King) 去世，享年 59 岁
item.description:
芝加哥（美联社）——史黛西·金（Stacey King）去世了。1991-93 年，他随芝加哥公牛队连续三届夺得 NBA 冠军，之后又以赢得艾美奖、深受球迷喜爱的播音员身份重返公牛队。他59岁了。
公牛队宣布金周日去世，并表示他们接到了家人的通知。目前还没有其他细节。
公牛队主席杰里·雷因斯多夫在一份声明中表示：“史黛西·金是公牛队大家庭中一位珍贵的成员，也是我们组织历史上真正独特的人物之一。”“他与芝加哥、公牛队和我们球迷的联系跨越了三十多年——首先是作为一名球员，后来作为明确无误的声音帮助公牛队篮球走进了几代球迷的家中。我们将深深地怀念他，并记住他每天给我们的组织、我们的广播和我们的球迷带来的欢乐、活力、幽默、坦率和热情。我们的思念与他的家人和亲人在一起。”
金在公牛队开始了他的 NBA 职业生涯，公牛队在 1989 年选秀中以第六顺位选中了这位身高 6 英尺 11 英寸的前锋/中锋。在公牛队的五个赛季中，他场均贡献 6.6 分和 3.3 个篮板。
他在职业生涯中总共在 NBA 打了八个赛季，其中还曾效力于明尼苏达州、迈阿密、波士顿和达拉斯。金职业生涯场均贡献6.4分和3.3个篮板。
作为一名广播员，金深受新一代公牛队支持者的喜爱。他担任公牛队比赛解说员长达二十多年。
“史黛西喜欢成为公牛队的一员，”公牛队总裁兼首席执行官迈克尔·雷因斯多夫在一份声明中表示。“你可以在他所做的每一件事中感受到这一点——他打球的方式、他对比赛的方式以及他与我们球迷联系的方式。他有一种独特的天赋，能够将人们聚集在一起，让每场比赛都变得个性化。他在每次转播中都带来了对比赛的能量和热爱，帮助球迷感受到与我们球队的联系。无论是通过转播、对话还是与球迷合影，史黛西都让人们感到被重视和被重视。
“我们很幸运不仅以球员和解说员的身份认识他，而且以朋友的身份认识他。史黛西真诚地关心他人，他让我们的组织变得更好。我们将深深地想念他，他的影响、记忆和遗产将永远成为芝加哥公牛队的一部分。”
芝加哥体育网总裁兼首席执行官迈克尔·麦卡锡称金是“芝加哥体育界最受欢迎的人物之一”。
“史黛西拥有将几代公牛队球迷联系在一起的独特能力，”麦卡锡说。“无论是通过他的篮球洞察力、他的幽默，还是他令人难忘的电话，他让每场比赛都变得更加有趣，每一次转播都变得更好。”
金用他令人难忘的电话和绰号让粉丝们高兴不已。每当公牛队投出一记重球时，他的热情就显而易见。
“我们喜欢我们所做的事情，”金上个月在他的“给我辣酱”播客中说道，这是他在重大比赛后经常发表的评论。“这是一项有趣的工作。这对我来说似乎从来都不是工作。每天晚上，我都去工作，无论赢、输或平，我都很开心。”

item.title: 百老汇复兴剧和《解放》在 Pink 主办的托尼奖上大获全胜
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — “Schmigadoon!,” an adaptation ofan Apple TV seriesthat gently mocks big, brassy Broadway shows, won the best new musical Tony Award on a night when actorJohn Lithgowand playwright Bess Wohl made history.
The musical parodies golden-age Broadway classics like “The Music Man” and “Oklahoma!” centered on a modern-day couple finding themselves in a “Brigadoon”-like fantasyland where the wholesome townspeople keep breaking into song. The win is a redemption for creator Cinco Paul, whose TV series was canceled after two seasons. He won Tonys for the score and the book Sunday.
“Sometimes singing, dancing, jokes and a happy ending are all you need,” said producer Lorne Michaels, the creator of “Saturday Night Live,” after the win.
The win for “Schmigadoon!” also completes what some unofficially call a “studio EGOT,” giving the producing company credits for winning awards at all four major ceremonies. Apple already has Emmys for comedies “Ted Lasso” and“The Studio,”the Oscar for best picture for “CODA” and a Grammy via Chris Stapleton’s contribution to the “F1” soundtrack.
The prize for the best new play went to Wohl’s“Liberation,”about a consciousness-raising women’s group in 1970s Ohio, which earlier this year also won thePulitzer Prize for drama.
Wohl’s play collects stories from second-wave feminists from all walks of life as they tackle misogyny, racism and traditional gender roles. Wohl is only the fourth woman to win a best play Tony, joining Wendy Wasserstein, Yasmina Reza and Frances Goodrich.
Bess Wohl, center, accepts the award for best play for “Liberation” during the 79th Tony Awards on Sunday, June 7, 2026, at Radio City Music Hall in New York. (Photo by Charles Sykes/Invision/AP)
“I want to honor women everywhere who have the courage to use their voice,” said Wohl, who thanked her mom, daughters and female producers. “And to all the girls out there: May you speak your truth, and may the world be wise enough to listen.”
“Liberation” joins a list of 18 plays that have won the Pulitzer Prize for drama and theTony Awardin the same year.
The Tony for best play revival went to“Death of a Salesman,”Arthur Miller’s masterpiece that looks at the unraveling of the American Dream. It won the 1949 Tony for best new play and best revival crowns in 1984, 1999 and 2012.
It earned a leading six Tonys: “Roseanne” star Laurie Metcalf won her third Tony for playing Willy Loman’s wife opposite Nathan Lane in the revival, which also won for lighting, scenic design and sound design. Joe Mantello won best director for a play.
“Ragtime”— a big, soaring musical that depicts an America being remade by immigration, racial violence, industrial wealth and political unrest — won the best musical revival.
Caissie Levy, who was Broadway’s first Elsa in “Frozen,” won her first Tony for playing the matriarch of a wealthy suburban family in “Ragtime.” She thanks all the babysitters who let her become both a mother and a Broadway performer.
Moments later, Joshua Henry, a four-time nominee, won his first Tony as Coalhouse Walker Jr. in “Ragtime.” “Even in the face of pain and tragedy, he found a way to be heard,” Henry said of his character. “Every artist in this room, every artist at home, fight — fight to be heard
Joshua Henry accepts the award for best performance by an actor in a leading role in a musical for “Ragtime” during the 79th Tony Awards on Sunday, June 7, 2026, at Radio City Music Hall in New York. (Photo by Charles Sykes/Invision/AP)
Lithgow won his third Tonyfor best lead actor in a play for “Giant” playing children’s writerRoald Dahlin Mark Rosenblatt’s production set in 1983, when the author is facing intense backlash for his antisemitic comments. At 80, he is the oldest man ever to win a competitive acting Tony.
“Two Tony bookends with 53 years between them,” he said. “In those years, I have worked with hundreds of just fantastic theater artists. I’ve had dozens and dozens of ecstatic moments on the stage, but I have to tell you right now, this moment has got to be one of the best.”
Shoshana Bean, who won best featured actress in a musical for playing a single mom in “The Lost Boys,” echoed the themes of “Liberation” in her speech.
“This is for the mamas. This is for the single mamas. This is for my single mama. You are the wild heroes. This is for the incredible army of women that surround and uplift me,” she said.
“Cats: The Jellicle Ball,”which reimagines the 1980s classic feline musical as a celebration of queer ballroom culture, won for best direction of a musical by Zhailon Levingston and Bill Rauch.
“We honor the Black and brown trans women and gay men who were ballroom’s pioneers, as well as today’s icons, and our cast of astonishing triple-threats, including people from their 20s to their 80s, and every decade in between,” Levingston said.
Lesley Manville, an Oscar nominee for “Phantom Thread,” won best lead actress in a play, making her Broadway debut in a modern retelling of Sophocles’ classic tragedy “Oedipus.”
Pink, the Tony’s host, started the show spinning and then dangling uncomfortably from a harness over the stage, dressed like Peter Pan. Former host Neil Patrick Harris stepped in to suggest the first-time host just be herself.
“You’re Pink, Pink. You can do anything,” he told her.
After lifting Harris off the stage with her legs, Pink relented to his suggestion, added a top hat and belted out “Lady Marmalade” with contributions from dozens of performers including Lea Michele and Megan Thee Stallion — and some 170 performers.
In her opening remarks, Pink, who has not yet gotten a Broadway credit, called herself theater’s second-biggest fan after her teenage daughter, Willow. “I’m not here just to steal peoples’ wigs, although I will be doing that. I’m here to celebrate the hardest-working people in show business,” she said.
Host Pink performs during the 79th Tony Awards on Sunday, June 7, 2026, at Radio City Music Hall in New York. (Photo by Charles Sykes/Invision/AP)
“Schmigadoon!” and “Death of a Salesman” each went into the main telecast with a lead of three Tonys after a pre-show on Pluto TV hosted by Laura Benanti and Tituss Burgess that announced the more technical awards. Qween Jean became the first openly trans Tony winner ever for making the costumes for “Cats: The Jellicle Ball.”
After the big opening number, Pink was generally a genial, exuberant figure, popping up in new outfits for a few self-deprecating bits, but then roaring to life when leading a very convincing “All That Jazz” as “Chicago” celebrated its 30th anniversary, with the pop singer nailing the Bob Fosse-inspired choreography.
The In Memoriam section was led by Leslie Odom Jr. singing a powerful “Without You” from “Rent,” honoring such lost figures as actors Robert Duvall, Robert Redford and Diane Keaton, as well as playwright Tom Stoppard. Rachel Zegler then stunned with a version of “What I Did For Love” from “A Chorus Line,” which last year celebrated its 50th anniversary.
Other performances included the original lead cast members of “The Book of Mormon” — Josh Gad, Andrew Rannells, Rory O’Malley and Nikki M. James — reuniting tocelebrate its 15th anniversary.
For more coverage of the 2026 Tony Awards, visithttps://apnews.com/hub/tony-awards.

item.title: 《恐怖电影》斩获票房冠军，击败《宇宙大师》并延续低预算势头
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——夏季票房正在蓬勃发展——但这并不是因为通常的嫌疑。
据周日电影公司估计，在独立恐怖电影占据票房冠军三周后，恐怖恶搞电影《恐怖电影》周末票房收入达到 5500 万美元，轻松击败了远非强大的《宇宙大师》。
电影院最近出现了新的秩序，Z世代的购票者纷纷涌向恐怖片《痴迷》和《密室》，这两部影片都是由 YouTuber 转型为电影制片人制作的。这些电影的光芒甚至超过了华特迪士尼公司的《星球大战：曼达洛人和格罗古》。
这个周末，喜剧是失败者的冠军。尽管这一类型的电影在影院中几乎已经消亡，但第六部《恐怖电影》在全球上映的票房收入为 1.055 亿美元，创系列最高纪录。韦恩斯兄弟的喜剧甚至超越了其主要讽刺目标“尖叫”系列。今年早些时候，《惊声尖叫7》全球首映票房为9700万美元。
尽管米拉麦克斯制作了新的“恐怖电影”，但这两个系列均由派拉蒙影业发行。这部续集由马龙、肖恩、基南和克雷格·韦恩斯共同编剧，标志着韦恩斯夫妇在 2001 年的《恐怖电影 2》后因创意分歧而退出该系列。
“对于这部喜剧续集来说，这是一个出色的开场，”电影咨询公司 FranchiseRe 的经营者大卫·A·格罗斯 (David A. Gross) 说道。“继 2013 年最后一集安娜·法瑞丝和雷吉娜·霍尔被排除在外之后，这是一个巨大的反弹。周末的数字是该类型平均水平的三倍。”
评论不好（烂番茄上有 26% 的新鲜度），观众评分（CinemaScore 为“B”）也一般。但这并没有阻止这部耗资 3000 万美元的《恐怖电影》在预算更大的竞争中占据主导地位。
根据 20 世纪 80 年代动画系列和美泰玩具改编的剑与魔法动作冒险片《宇宙大师》未能重振这个休眠系列。亚马逊米高梅发行的《宇宙大师》电影是继 1987 年同名电影之后的第二部，国内首映票房为 2,930 万美元。
由尼古拉斯·加利津 (Nicholas Galitzine) 饰演希曼 (He-Man) 主演的《宇宙大师》(Masters of the Universe) 海外票房收入达 2500 万美元。但对于一部制作成本近 2 亿美元的电影来说，需要更高的发行量才能实现盈利。
这是美泰工作室自 2023 年推出《芭比娃娃》以来的首个作品。但在该电影获得 14.5 亿美元的巨大成功之后，对于这家玩具公司来说，《宇宙大师》将更接近失败。
A24 的《Backrooms》是上周末的热门影片，但在第二个周末急剧下滑，跌幅达 68%，票房为 2,590 万美元。但根据 20 岁的凯恩·帕森 (Kane Parson) 的 YouTube 系列片改编的耗资 1000 万美元的电影《幕后密室》(Backrooms) 仍然创下了票房纪录。目前，它是 A24 有史以来票房最高的电影，全球票房达到 2.12 亿美元，超过了《马蒂至尊》。
焦点影业出品的《Obsession》在第四周末的票房收入为 2560 万美元，几乎并列第三。26 岁的库里·巴克 (Curry Barker) 的恐怖片票房较上周末下降了 7%。不考虑通货膨胀，没有一部恐怖电影的第四周末表现比这更好。
《痴迷》讲述了一个男人希望他的暗恋对象回报他的感情的故事，制作成本不到 100 万美元。目前，该片的国内票房为 1.521 亿美元，全球票房为 2.248 亿美元，创下了《焦点》的纪录。
在上映的第三周末，《曼达洛人和格罗古》以 1000 万美元的票房一路跌至第六位。它甚至被 Fathom Entertainment 的《神奇数字马戏团：最后一幕》击败，该动画系列的最后两集结合在一起。它筹集了 1270 万美元。
狮门影业的迈克尔·杰克逊传记片《迈克尔》以 8.98 亿美元的全球票房成为该工作室有史以来票房最高的电影。在不考虑通货膨胀的情况下，这使得它在该工作室的《暮光之城》和《饥饿游戏》系列中的票房最高的作品中遥遥领先。
2026年，环球影业迎来了首部票房十亿美元的电影。《超级马里奥银河电影》全球票房突破10亿美元。
根据 Comscore 的数据，整个周末的房价比去年同一个周末上涨了 63%。全年门票销量增长超过 13%。下周末，史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格的《披露日》首映。
根据 Comscore 的数据，随着周一公布的最终国内数据，该列表考虑了美国和加拿大影院周五至周日的预计门票销售情况：
2.《宇宙大师》，2930万美元。
5.《神奇数字马戏团：最后一幕》，1270万美元。
6.《星球大战：曼达洛人和格罗古》，1000万美元。
10.《穿普拉达的女王2》，280万美元。

item.title: A select list of winners at the 2026 Tony Awards
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——以下是 2026 年托尼奖的部分获奖者。
改编自 Apple TV 电视剧的《Schmigadoon!》赢得了最佳新音乐剧奖，80 岁的演员约翰·利思戈 (John Lithgow) 成为有史以来赢得托尼奖最佳表演奖的最年长男性。（6月8日）

item.title: 扬声器坏了？挑剔的拉链？反消费主义维修咖啡馆敦促你修理它而不是推销它
item.description:
NEW PALTZ, N.Y. (AP) — On a drizzly Saturday morning late last month, the basement of the New Paltz United Methodist Church filled with old lamps, blunt knives, malfunctioning sound mixers and balky zippers.
About a dozen volunteers welcomed the broken goods and their owners to a worldwide movement that’s evangelizing new relationships between people and their things.
Repair Cafes — free events where volunteers with technical know-how help neighbors fix myriad household items — are part of a new brand ofanticonsumerismthat’s trying to offer analternativeto the mass-produced disposable goods that have dominated the global economy for the last half-century. Helping fuel that move to repairing, not buying, areU.S. consumer prices, which climbed sharply again last month as thewar with Irandelivered higher gasoline prices andmore painfor Americans.
After starting in theNetherlandswith a single event in 2009, Repair Cafe has grown into a global nonprofit with more than 59,000 members, some 4,000 cafes and close to 850,000 items fixed a year.
“We need to change our mindset. We need to change the economy,” Repair Cafe founder Martine Postma said. “Even if Repair Cafes can’t solve the problem alone, then still they are a very clear sign that change is needed on a much higher level.”
In New Paltz, a Hudson Valley college town about two hours from New York, 50 people brought about 85 items to the Repair Cafe: an antique fan that required rewiring, shirts, pants, jackets, stuffed animals. There were old family photos that needed restoring and jewelry awaiting work like restringing beads or replacing clasps.
Repair experts waited behind long cafeteria tables to teach alternatives, giving people chances to learn that flawed goods aren’t automatically junk.
“Maybe their initial reason for coming is monetary or sentimental,” organizer Holly Shader said.
More than that, she added, “it gives people a chance to work together and extend the life of something. People form relationships.”
This article is part of AP’s Be Well coverage, focusing on wellness, fitness, diet and mental health.Read more Be Well.
The experts on hand fixed 71 of the items, found that four needed more work and deemed 10 beyond repair. They said they volunteer for the low-pressure joy of fixing things, with networking as a side benefit.
“I get to come and actually do the work and meet the nice people and show them how to put something together,” contractor Patrick L. Murphy said.
The Buy Nothing Project, “right to repair” legislation, and a growing number of tool libraries also are dedicated to repairing, trading, and giving instead of buying and selling.
Starting in Washington state in 2013, the Buy Nothing Project maintains an app and social media presence that links people giving things away with people nearby who want them — a worldwide network of gift economies, as described on its Facebook page.
Founder Liesl Clark said the network has expanded to at least 12.5 million people on Facebook, showing a growth rate with the ability to influence corporate and state behavior.
“What was a social movement has really become a safety net for millions of people,” she said. “People are seeing that you don’t have to go to the Amazons of the world to get what you might need, there is a robust material culture in your community.
“We want to change the way that the world consumes.”
The movement “started as a social and economic and environmental experiment,” she noted.
“There’s going to be a conversation that you have, when you and someone else are fixing something together,” she said. “We’re finding that we’re crossing a lot of barriers.”
In today’s modern, disposable culture, many people have lost the ability to repair household goods, a skill that was once nearly universal, said Peter Counter, an engineer who’s studying Repair Cafes and working on a doctorate at the University for the Creative Arts in Farnham, England.
“The idea that you can fix your own stuff has receded because the skills are not being passed down,” he said. “If you want something fixed, it’s almost certainly cheaper to go buy a new one.”
Community repair is thriving, Counter said, because it is volunteers who spend their time, making it financially viable even if you need to buy spare parts.
The “right to repair” movement wants consumers to be empowered to repair their own products instead of being forced to go to the manufacturer for tools and instructions. A national campaign pushed in 2023 for states to consider bills requiring manufacturers to give access to tools and instructions for both customers and repair shops. A handful of states have passed legislation.
And around the country, some jurisdictions are hosting tool libraries that allow people to borrow expensive tools just like library books.
In New Paltz, Paula Weinstein, 79, brought in a 1930s-era Hammond clock and handed it to Bob Morton.
Morton — an 82-year-old former IBM electrical engineer — said he enjoys using his skills to stay intellectually busy and help people.
“I’ve been blessed to still have a brain,” the grandfather of three said. “It’s a chance to do something.”
Weinstein added, “It’s wonderful to see people restoring older things.”
After hours of patient work together, the hands of her clock moved.
“Yes, it’s working!” she shouted. “Oh, my goodness, thank you!”

item.title: 人工智能股票收复了上周的部分抛售，而油价则从隔夜高点回落
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——随着周一股市在人工智能热潮中反弹，华尔街正在收复周五以来的部分抛售。与此同时，在以色列和伊朗之间的战斗之后，油价走高，但已从隔夜的高点回落。
标准普尔 500 指数上涨 0.8%，较上周五下跌 2.6%，为 10 月份以来的最大跌幅。截至东部时间上午 10:30，道琼斯工业平均指数上涨 150 点，即 0.3%，纳斯达克综合指数上涨 1.3%。
一些表现最好的公司是销售计算机芯片、内存和其他产品的公司，这些产品推动了人工智能的繁荣。由于担心人工智能的狂热导致其价格过高，周五它们的股价暴跌。这些担忧拖累韩国 Kospi 指数周一早盘下跌 8.3%，令三星电子和 SK 海力士等科技股遭受重创。
但随着交易通过欧洲向西转移到纽约，价格回升。美光科技 (Micron Technology) 周五下跌 13.3%，创标准普尔 500 指数最大跌幅，随后上涨 8.6%。该公司股价在 2026 年迄今已上涨两倍多，随后又恢复了上涨势头。
标准普尔道琼斯指数表示，Marvell Technology 的股价已增长到足以加入其广泛关注的标准普尔 500 指数，该公司在其首次交易中股价上涨 11.9%。今年迄今为止，Marvell 股价在上周某一天飙升 32.5% 的推动下也上涨了两倍多。这是自 2000 年开始交易以来表现最好的一天，此前 Nvidia 首席执行官黄仁勋在台湾的一次会议上表示 Marvell 可能成为“下一个万亿美元公司”。
这样的评论可能会立即使一家公司的价值增加数十亿美元，这让批评者认为人工智能股票已经太热了。芯片和内存公司确实因为人工智能的繁荣而获得了收入和利润的大幅增长，但它们的股价却以惊人的速度飙升。例如，截至周四，广受关注的半导体股指数今年迄今已飙升近 85%。
现在的问题是，周五的下跌是否是经济低迷的开始，还是只是一个暂停，动摇了过度乐观的情绪。
摩根士丹利策略师迈克尔·威尔逊相对乐观。他在一份报告中写道：“市场很少以自三月份低点以来的速度直线波动。”“我们认为，如果这次牛市要延续到年底，调整是不可避免的，而且最终是健康的”，并将标准普尔 500 指数拉至 8,000 点的基准目标。这将比周五收盘价上涨 8.3%。
亚马逊宣布达成一项价值数十亿美元的交易，康宁将为其全国各地的数据中心生产光纤、电缆和其他产品，康宁股价上涨 7.6%。
这帮助抵消了金宝汤 0.6% 的下跌，该公司报告最近一个季度的利润高于分析师预期，但收入下降幅度也更大。当 Marvell Technology 的股票加入标准普尔 500 指数时，该公司的股票也将退出标准普尔 500 指数。
在以色列和伊朗相互发动袭击、威胁将该地区拖入全面战争后，石油市场价格大幅上涨。国际标准布伦特原油价格隔夜短暂突破 98 美元。
但在伊朗军方表示将停止进攻行动后，局势随后有所缓和。布伦特原油价格最近为每桶 94.29 美元，较周五上涨 1.3%。
与伊朗的战争造成的高油价已经推高了通货膨胀，这不仅增加了家庭开支，还增加了债券市场的收益率。全球范围内的高收益率最近威胁到经济放缓，并压低股票和各种其他投资的价格。
继周五上涨后，周一美国国债收益率略有回落。10年期国债收益率从4.55%小幅降至4.54%
在国外股市，继亚洲股市大幅下跌后，欧洲股指小幅走低。
日本政府将今年第一季度的年化经济增长率修正为 1.8%，低于此前预测的 2.1%，日本日经 225 指数下跌 3.8%。
上海股市和香港股市也分别下跌 1.7% 和 1.2%。
美联社商业作家 Yuri Kageyama 和 Matt Ott 对本报告做出了贡献。
